---
title: "starting_out"
author: "anuj s"
date: "2026-02-24"
output: html_document
---

```{r warning = F, message = F}

## packages
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(AmphiNom)
library(rgbif)
library(devtools)
library(rnaturalearth)
library(rnaturalearthhires)

## Retrieve the paths specific to the machine I am using.
host <- Sys.info()[["nodename"]]

if (host == "ANUJ-PC") {
  base_path <- "C:/Users/Anuj Shinde/OneDrive - Australian National University"
} else if (host == "RSB-088702") {
  base_path <- "C:/Users/u8007407/OneDrive - Australian National University"
} else if (host == "rsb0001147") {
  base_path <- "onedrive://bf21b313-5537-4a7c-a787-0949e4040392/OneDrive/Project%20DataFiles/biodiv_data_gaps"
} else {
  stop("Unknown machine — please define paths for this system.")
}

## Set all paths (external or internal)
wd <- list()
wd$raw <- file.path(base_path, "Project DataFiles",
                     "biodiv_data_gaps","raw_files") #general folder
wd$data <- file.path(base_path, "Project DataFiles",
                     "biodiv_data_gaps","data") #general folder
wd$output <- file.path(base_path, "Project DataFiles",
                       "biodiv_data_gaps","output") #general folder
wd$starting_out <- file.path(base_path, "Project DataFiles",
                       "biodiv_data_gaps","starting_out") #script specific folder

sf::sf_use_s2(FALSE) # disables s2 geometry in the sf package.

```



```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
